titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robin Magowan Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday September 16, 2018 at 04:04

did.unittitle: Robin Magowan papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 26 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 27, 35 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robin Magowan (b. 1936) is an American poet, memoirist, and travel writer. He studied at Exeter, Harvard (B. A.) and Columbia (M.A.) and Yale, where he earned a Ph.D. in Comparative Literature. He taught at the University of Washington, and the University of California, Berkeley. In 1986 he founded the journal Margin; he served as editor until 1990. He is the author of books in several genres, including: Lilac Cigarette in a Wish Cathedral , Memoirs of a Minotaur, And Other Voyages, Fabled Cities: Samarkand, Bukhara, Khiva, Kings of the Road Portrait of Racers and Racing, Birds in a Forest Swaying , and The Rim of Dawn . He is the nephew of poet James Merrill.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robin Magowan Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Robin Magowan Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five groupings: April 2012 Acquisition; July 2014 Acquisition; February 2016 Acquisition; September 2016 Acquisition; February 2017 Acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collecton consists of correspondence, writings, notebooks, printed material, audiovisual materials, electronic media, and other papers documenting the life and work of poet and writer Robin Magowan. Writings include manuscripts and typescripts of poems and prose, including drafts and corrected copies of Lilac Cigarette in a Wish Cathedral (University of South Carolina Press, 1998), Memoirs of a Minotaur (Story Line Press, 1999), and other works. Includes correspondence and other papers relating to Magowan's uncle, James Merrill.

did.unittitle: April 2012 Acquisition
Publisher, family, and college correspondence; printed materials
Correspondence and printed material
Correspondence, primarily from family, including Magowan's father
Correspondence from Magowan's mother and others
Photocopies of James Merrill correspondence from University of St. Louis, Missouri and elsewhere
Correspondence, printed material relating to art and gardening, and printed ephemera
Typescripts including Green Dizzy Screaming Sun-Abyss; Harvard graduation material

Publishing agreements, photographs, family genealogy, family correspondence, and typescripts including Maya Revisited, Paul Clouded, Lilac Cigarette in a Wish Cathedral, The Wanton Current, and others
Corrected typescripts for Lilac Cigarette in a Wish Cathedral and other writings
Corrected typescripts and photocopies
Photocopies of typescript of Maya Revisited and other works, clippings
Typescripts for Fabled Cities of Central Asia, and other works, subject files, correspondence, wedding photograph, clippings, and a wooden box and coins
Photocopies of typescripts and other papers
Photocopies of typescripts and other papers
Writings including Trinidad, Birds, Connecticut, A Robin's life, Collected Incongruities, and poems
Writings, including poems, prose relating to gardening, and manuscript of autobiography ("The Minotaur")
Typescripts including The Wanton Current and others
Notebooks, astrological charts, baby book for Robert Anderson Magowan III, and master's thesis
Notebooks, bachelor's thesis, address book, and writings
Notebooks, travel notes, "The Dream Book," year book, poems, scrapbook, and journals
Notebooks including poetry, diaries, Japan and central Asia travel notes, and translations; typescripts; manuscripts; postcard
Notebooks kept in London and elsewhere
Notebooks including drafts of poems and prose, clippings, typescripts, and college coursework
Notebooks, writings, including drafts of poems, typescripts, and clippings
"Planters" notebook
Printed materials, typescripts, manuscripts, and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials include ephemera, newsletters, clippings, and James Merril materials. Typescripts and manuscripts include The Poems of Jeufre Rudel, Literary Criticism of Andre Gide, and A Different Person. Correspondence includes letters and postcards from Sonta Angelo, Micaela di Leonardo, Mark O'Brian, and others.

did.unittitle: Planter's notebook, newspapers, Harvard graduation materials
Audiovisual materials

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note with the audiocassette reads: Dear Robin and Juliet, I hope you enjoy this tape. With appreciation and affection, Helen.
